result.stdout:
Usage: [command] [arguments...] [options...] [-h|--help] [--version]

Runs the specified DecSm.Atom project with the given arguments.
         This is the default command executed when no specific subcommand is provided.

Arguments:
  [0] <string[]?>    Optional. Arguments to pass directly to the DecSm.Atom build project.
         These arguments are typically used to specify targets or parameters for the build.

Options:
  --project <string?>    Optional. The name of the DecSm.Atom project or file-based app to run. Defaults to "_atom".
         This allows specifying which build definition to execute within a multi-project solution.
  --file <string?>       Optional. The path to a C# file to run as a file-based DecSm.Atom application.

Commands:
  nuget-add    Adds a NuGet package source with the specified name and URL to the global NuGet configuration.

commands.payload:
Usage: [command] [arguments...] [options...] [-h|--help] [--version]

Runs the specified DecSm.Atom project with the given arguments.
         This is the default command executed when no specific subcommand is provided.

Arguments:
  [0] <string[]?>    Optional. Arguments to pass directly to the DecSm.Atom build project.
         These arguments are typically used to specify targets or parameters for the build.

Options:
  --project <string?>    Optional. The name of the DecSm.Atom project or file-based app to run. Defaults to "_atom".
         This allows specifying which build definition to execute within a multi-project solution.
  --file <string?>       Optional. The path to a C# file to run as a file-based DecSm.Atom application.

Commands:
  nuget-add    Adds a NuGet package source with the specified name and URL to the global NuGet configuration.

result.stdout:
Usage: nuget-add [options...] [-h|--help] [--version]

Adds a NuGet package source with the specified name and URL to the global NuGet configuration.

Options:
  --name <string>    The unique name to assign to the new NuGet package source. [Required]
  --url <string>     The URL of the NuGet feed to add (e.g., "https://api.nuget.org/v3/index.json"). [Required]

commands.payload:
Usage: nuget-add [options...] [-h|--help] [--version]

Adds a NuGet package source with the specified name and URL to the global NuGet configuration.

Options:
  --name <string>    The unique name to assign to the new NuGet package source. [Required]
  --url <string>     The URL of the NuGet feed to add (e.g., "https://api.nuget.org/v3/index.json"). [Required]
